nycFatalities.fatalitySource: NYC Open Data — NYPD Motor Vehicle Collisions

waymoSafety.waymoRideshareShareSource: Waymo reached ~22% of the San Francisco rideshare market by end of 2024

rideHailVMTShare.note: Ride-hail share of city-core VMT. The 2.0% effective VMT share is derived from NYC ride-hail VMT (~9%, Open Plans/Streetsblog 2023) x observed Waymo rideshare penetration in SF (~22%).

waymo.source: Kusano et al., Traffic Injury Prevention (2025), 56.7M miles

rideHailSafetyComparison.caveat: Methodologies differ across these sources. Waymo data uses police-reported incidents matched to location baselines; Uber/Lyft figures are self-reported. Direct comparison should be interpreted with caution.

studies.name: Kusano et al. (2024)
studies.finding: 85% fewer any-injury-reported crashes over 7.1M autonomous miles

studies.name: Kusano et al. (2025)
studies.finding: 85% fewer suspected serious injury+ crashes, 79% fewer any-injury-reported crashes over 56.7M miles

studies.name: Swiss Re / Waymo (2024)
studies.finding: Waymo vehicles had zero bodily injury claims vs. human baseline

permitTimeline.note: Testing with safety drivers only — Governor blocked commercial operations

timeline.event: NYC DOT grants Waymo first AV testing permit
timeline.detail: Up to 8 Jaguar I-PACEs in Manhattan and Downtown Brooklyn, safety driver required

timeline.event: Testing permit extended through end of 2025
timeline.detail: Limited testing continues — no public ride-hail service allowed

timeline.event: Governor Hochul proposes robotaxis statewide — except NYC
timeline.detail: NYC explicitly excluded from commercial AV operations
Labor opposition from taxi unions kills even the statewide plan
No legal path to commercial AV service exists in NYC

timeline.event: Still blocked — the most restrictive major US city
timeline.detail: No legislation, no commercial permit, no driverless operations allowed

sources.name: NHTSA FARS

sources.description: NYPD motor vehicle collision and fatality data, updated daily

sources.description: New York City traffic safety initiative and crash data

sources.name: Kusano et al. (2024)
sources.description: Peer-reviewed study: 85% fewer any-injury-reported crashes in 7.1M Waymo miles

sources.name: Swiss Re / Waymo Insurance Study (2024)
sources.description: Zero bodily injury claims for Waymo fleet vs. human baseline

sources.name: Fehr & Peers Ride-Hail VMT Study (2019)
sources.description: Ride-hail accounts for 1-13% of city-core VMT across major US cities

sources.name: Kusano et al. (2025) — 56.7M Miles
sources.description: Updated peer-reviewed study: 85% fewer suspected serious injury+ crashes over 56.7M Waymo miles

sources.name: YipitData / App Economy Insights — Waymo SF Market Share
sources.description: Waymo reached ~22% of the San Francisco rideshare market by end of 2024 (YipitData via a16z)

sources.description: Mayor Adams and DOT announce approval of first autonomous vehicle testing application
